title: What It Takes To Get Rich In Your 20s
author: Dan Koe

sections.title: How to actually get rich
sections.content: 00:00 The only way to get rich is to one value your time at a delusional dollar amount, 00:06 to be obsessed with getting rich, and three, 00:09 give yourself no other option but to get rich. 00:13 That's what this entire video is about, is getting rich and the truth behind it. 00:18 And now I know there are going to be people in the back, 00:21 who are like, money is in everything. 00:23 Or they're regurgitating, 00:24 some shallow advice that they heard about how money doesn't, 00:27 buy happiness that they got from someone who doesn't have money nor happiness. 00:32 But this video isn't for those who just decided, 00:35 to click on this video and get angry and completely waste, 00:39 their time. 00:39 This video is for those who want to get unapologetically rich, 00:44 not because you're a terrible or an evil person, 00:47 but because the only other option in your head, 00:50 is to be valued at what the market thinks you're worth. 00:53 And that's terrifying. 00:55 For myself, 00:55 I realized pretty early in my life, 00:57 that I was not okay with breaking my back for 8 to 10 hours, 01:01 a day to make 80 to $150,000 a year in a job. 01:05 where I own nothing, doing work I don't care about, 01:08 especially when I can see as clear, 01:10 as day that there are people doing meaningful work, 01:13 who make 100 x that amount while working a fraction of the time. 01:17 I may not have had that much value to offer when I was young, 01:20 but I knew it was possible because I could just look, I could just observe. 01:25 I could just see that other people had a life that I could potentially want, 01:30 and if other people had done it, 01:31 then that means there is a series of steps that I can personally take, 01:35 especially in this world of technology and the internet and social media. 01:40 I could take a series of steps to achieve close to the same thing. 01:43 Not even the full thing, but I could at least have some control over my money. 01:48 Therefore, I could have some control over my day, 01:52 my routine, my mind, my emotions, and especially my time. 01:57 And the thing here is that the people who endlessly moralize, 02:02 or spiritualize money can't fathom a life where they aren't bound to it. 02:07 Now, the unfortunate thing with this video, 02:09 is that I can't tell you exactly what to do. 02:11 I mean, I can I can tell you what I did right. 02:14 That's what anyone can do. 02:15 Nobody can tell you what to do. 02:17 They can only tell you what they did. 02:19 But instead, I want to give you something that I think is more helpful. 02:23 I want to give you three ways to rewire how your mind thinks about money, 02:28 because that's what you need to make the right decisions, 02:32 to get rich or make a lot of money, or achieve really, 02:35 anything that you want. I don't want to give you the way. 02:38 I want to give you the tools that will help you create your own way, 02:42 so that it's sustainable. 02:44 In other words, 02:44 I want to install the beliefs in your mind, 02:47 that allow you to test the inputs that are much more likely, 02:52 to spit wealth out of reality as the output.

sections.title: The aspirational hourly rate
sections.content: 02:55 The first and most practical way to prime, 02:58 your mind to get rich is to adopt an aspirational hourly rate. 03:03 Now, this is a concept that I got from naval rather can't. 03:06 So I'll start with a quote from him, 03:08 which says set and enforce an aspirational hourly rate. 03:11 If fixing a problem will save less than your rate, ignore it. 03:15 If outsourcing a task will cost less than your hourly rate, outsource it. 03:19 My aspirational rate was $5,000 an hour. 03:23 Now, this will honestly baffle most people, 03:25 but I'm at a point and I try to say this without sounding arrogant. 03:29 That's kind of impossible online, 03:31 but just know that I try to say this without sounding arrogant, 03:34 but I've reached a point where someone could offer me $5,000, 03:39 for an hour of my time, and I wouldn't do it. 03:43 Not because I couldn't use the money or that I'm, 03:45 better than the money, but because there is time, focus, 03:49 and an obligation to deliver $5,000 worth of value in an hour on the line. 03:54 And that's a big responsibility that pulls my time, 03:59 focus, energy away from things that could potentially, 04:02 make me more than $5,000 an hour, 04:05 since I have the knowledge to do so. 04:07 It would be stupid of me to do that instead of the thing, 04:10 that would not only have more impact on more people rather than just one person, 04:15 because I know exactly what to do, 04:18 that would make me that much or more in an hour. 04:21 And that's the exact purpose of the aspirational hourly rate. It's not. 04:25 So you just magically make your aspirational hourly rate. 04:29 This isn't about manifesting. 04:30 This is about framing your mind, 04:32 to make the decisions that would lead to you acquiring the knowledge, 04:36 and taking the actions that would lead to you making, 04:39 that much an hour. 04:40 It helps your mind notice and choose the opportunities, 04:44 that put you in a place of leverage. 04:46 So let's imagine a pretty common scenario, right? 04:48 You purchase an item, 04:49 let's say off of Amazon for 50 bucks and it's defective or it's broken. 04:54 Are you going to return it or are you going to take the 30, 04:57 to 60 minutes to return it? 04:59 If so, you value your time at about $50 or less an hour, 05:04 because if you had the knowledge to make more money in that hour, 05:08 and the $50 that you lost wasn't a problem, then you would do so. 05:13 And this is where people with a poor relationship with money just go insane. 05:17 It's like, what, you're just going to waste $50? 05:19 Like, you purchase this? 05:20 Shouldn't you be mad at the seller for it? 05:22 Shouldn't you take it back and make sure they send you the right item? 05:26 Like to go through all of that, 05:28 and waste that mental energy? Time? Focus, attention. 05:31 That's important. 05:32 That is so much more valuable than a measly $50. 05:36 And going through all of that just to get something, 05:39 just reorder the item and make more money than that. 05:41 And I know I'm saying that in an arrogant way. 05:43 I'm just trying to get the point across as to, 05:46 something that you could do that is a better decision. 05:49 It's not a bad thing to not return an item, 05:54 that you spent money on, just so you can get that money back, 05:57 or get the same item instead. 05:59 It's an opportunity that shines a light on a more important fact, 06:03 that you aren't conscious of a problem that you can solve, 06:07 that would make you 50 to 500 to $1,000 an hour. 06:11 Because if you were, it wouldn't make sense to return that item. 06:15 Now, the thing here is that if your aspirational hourly rate doesn't seem, 06:20 impossibly high or delusional high, you're doing it wrong. 06:24 Why? 06:25 Because delusional goals rewire your brain. 06:28 Your brain is literally rewiring itself, 06:31 based on what you're thinking about and learning about right now. 06:34 This video included every single thing that you allow into your mind. 06:39 Shapes who you are and what you do now. 06:42 When you imagine a better future, it activates, 06:45 the same brain networks as if you were experiencing that future. 06:49 Every single moment of focus. 06:51 The tension is a chance to rewire your brain in alignment, 06:54 with the person you want to become. 06:56 That's one of many reasons that doom scholars, and people, 06:59 who don't actually pay attention, 07:01 to what they allow into their environment, both physical. 07:05 and digital, are digging their own grave one moment at a time. 07:09 And beyond that, 07:10 dopamine spikes more when you're in pursuit of a goal, 07:13 rather than when you achieve it. 07:15 But that's exactly where it gets dangerous, 07:17 because you can visualize your goals all day. 07:20 And that definitely helps with learning. 07:22 But you have to match that visualization, 07:25 with a level of challenge in reality to act and solidify, 07:30 that knowledge. 07:31 In other words, it's being in the game. 07:33 You have to be in the game if you want your visualization, 07:37 or your imagination to take any kind of form in reality. 07:41 Because if you don't, then that's where mental masturbation comes into play, 07:44 which is the act of telling everyone about your goals, 07:48 but never actually doing anything about it, 07:50 because you already got your dopamine hit from telling people about it. 07:54 The Golden zone for challenge. 07:55 And remember, challenge in the real world, 07:57 is how you solidify this visualization or this aspirational hourly rate. 08:03 And that challenge is that about 85% difficulty, 08:06 so not so high that you get anxious, but not so low that you get bored. 08:10 This is good because the stress from the progressive, 08:13 overload of challenge releases norepinephrine, 08:15 which enhances learning further and helps you adapt into that new state of mind. 08:19 So the entire point is that you can't just think about, 08:23 your aspirational hourly rate and expect to make more money. 08:26 You need to make difficult decisions with your rate in mind. 08:31 So the best way to start is just by not returning the $50 item, you'll be fine.

sections.title: Concentration of force is the only way to get rich
sections.content: 08:36 Now, the second point to help you with getting rich, 08:39 is that the concentration of force, 08:42 is the only way to get rich, 08:45 because if you want to get rich, 08:47 it needs to be your number one overwhelming desire. 08:50 I remember watching some of Ali Abdul's video on how to Get rich. 08:54 It's a video of his that did, 08:56 pretty good recently, and just in the first five minutes, 09:00 he talked, he felt bad kind of about saying it, 09:03 but he's like, honestly, if you want to get rich, 09:06 then you kind of have to be obsessed with it. 09:08 And when I first listened to that, I was like, that's not really true. 09:13 And then I sat with it and thought about it, 09:14 more and I'm like, actually, that's exactly what I did. 09:17 And personally, a moment of vulnerability here is that, 09:21 I feel like I've been kind of hiding from that. 09:24 I was kind of obsessed with making money for a long time, 09:27 but I never really registered it as that because that just sounds so shallow. 09:32 Oh, you have to become obsessed with getting rich. 09:35 In reality, I was learning, I was growing, I was acquiring these skills. 09:39 I was becoming a better person. 09:41 That's what getting rich is for in a lot of cases, 09:45 is becoming a good and better person to the point where you have so much value, 09:50 that you can't stop attracting wealth. 09:54 So for those who have this, like hang up in their mind of like, oh, 09:57 getting rich is bad, right? 09:59 That's just the direct connection. 10:00 The Direct Association Society has been telling me, 10:03 my parents have been telling me, and my pastor has been telling me, 10:06 everyone's been telling me, 10:07 oh, you don't need to get rich, you don't need to do that. 10:09 And somewhere in the back of your mind, you realize, and you know what? 10:13 What do you mean? 10:14 I'm learning these skills. I'm doing great things. 10:17 I'm helping other people, whether it be for business or really anything else. 10:21 I'm exchanging value. 10:23 But you, the one who is projecting these beliefs on me, 10:26 I have this notion that it's it's for some reason, evil, 10:30 to engage in a mutually beneficial transaction. 10:35 And I think that says a lot more about them than it does you. 10:39 So we have to become obsessed with getting rich. 10:42 We need concentration of force because you do this, 10:46 until the point of it becoming second nature to you. 10:50 Think of a basketball player, right? 10:51 They don't. They practice so much. 10:53 They've been obsessed with basketball for so long, 10:56 that it doesn't take any mental energy or really any effort, 11:00 at all to shoot three pointers over and over again. 11:03 They have like a 99% success rate with hitting a three pointer. 11:07 And if getting rich is synonymous, 11:09 with becoming a professional basketball player, 50%, 11:13 making 50% of the shots isn't going to cut it right. 11:16 They're never going to make it into the NBA. 11:18 You have to give it your all. 11:20 You have to become obsessed with it so that you can make it pro. 11:24 You can still make a lot of money. 11:25 There's a spectrum here, right? 11:27 There's super extraordinarily rich and then there's well-off. 11:30 And both goals are fine. 11:32 But if your goal is to get rich, 11:34 and that's a perfectly viable goal. 11:36 because it's not about the outcome, it's about the journey. 11:39 It's about who you become in the process. 11:40 Then that's what you need to do. 11:42 You need to be obsessed with it in order to reach the highest level. 11:44 Now the question here is like, okay, well, what about going out? 11:48 What about quote unquote enjoying life? 11:50 What about being social, 11:52 or just in general chasing the Western notion of happiness, 11:55 which is this just these endless dopamine hits that create euphoria? 11:59 That's not what happiness is. 12:00 Happiness is being fine. 12:02 It's being calm. 12:03 It's being in a good mood. It's not euphoria. 12:06 Well, the thing is, you can obviously do those, but, 12:09 you're not going to get wealthy because it's not your priority. 12:13 You are prioritizing those things over what, 12:16 it takes to reach the goal of getting wealthy. 12:20 And that's okay. 12:21 You don't need to have the goal of getting rich, 12:23 but many people do, and that's valid. 12:26 You don't need to project your goals and your beliefs on those people. 12:29 Someone without that as their priority isn't going to get rich. 12:34 That doesn't mean that you can't invest a bit of your money into the stock, 12:38 market over 40 years and see some kind of wealth in your future, 12:42 but the people with the goal of getting rich, 12:44 don't want it to come 40 years later, 12:47 because they realize that if they have the knowledge to do so, they can do it, 12:52 30 years earlier than that. 12:53 Another thing here is that getting rich as a priority, 12:56 doesn't make your life worse, like many people think. 12:59 It actually makes your life infinitely better because just think about it. 13:03 Does a professional athlete have a terrible life? 13:06 Probably not, because they're doing what they love. 13:09 Most of the time they're at this level of challenge. 13:12 They're competing. They're playing a game. 13:14 They're having fun. 13:16 Their entire life revolves around their main priority, 13:19 and that's not a bad thing. 13:20 You don't see a bodybuilder hating their life for eating healthy food, 13:25 and being disciplined in the gym. 13:26 That's what they want to do. 13:28 That's what makes them happy. 13:30 Just because they don't play video games all day, 13:32 or eat junk food, or engage in the, 13:35 the finer things in life that you think are, 13:38 good, doesn't mean they aren't happy. 13:40 In fact, they're probably much happier than the average of the population. 13:44 Because if you understand psychology and flow states and what singular focus, 13:49 the enjoyment that that brings into your life, 13:51 then you understand what I'm saying here. 13:53 The problem here is that most people don't apply that logic to business. 13:57 They see a professional athlete and they think, oh, he's living his best life. 14:01 But then they see a businessman who's dedicated his life, 14:04 to performing well as an athlete, as an intellectual athlete, 14:09 you could say in business, 14:11 and you don't think that is in the same realm, 14:14 as being a professional athlete, 14:15 now, making getting rich your one true priority doesn't mean that you just stop, 14:20 doing other things in life. 14:21 There's obviously things that you're going to have to do aside. 14:24 from trying to get rich, but that pain of not wanting to do, 14:29 those things are a way to orient your decision making. 14:33 Because the more you tolerate something in your life, 14:36 the more okay you are with not changing it. 14:39 So to make this tangible and to guide your decision making more, 14:43 I want you to create a list of things that you just don't do. 14:47 And these are things that are literally just not worth your time, 14:50 based on your aspirational hourly rate. 14:52 So this is coffee meetings or returning items or scrolling, 14:57 because you're bored, or partying because you lack novelty and stimulation, 15:01 or even things like texting everyone back on time. 15:04 Right? That is something that you do not need to do. 15:06 We aren't wired to instantly respond to people, 15:09 and we definitely aren't wired, 15:11 for people to have access to our mind at any time that they see fit. 15:15 Put your phone on, do not disturb and check it when you want to. 15:19 So when these things come up, 15:20 you just have to firmly state to yourself or the person involved. 15:24 I don't do that. 15:25 After that, you need to create a list of things that you will do. 15:29 These are things that merge, 15:30 your real hourly rate with your aspirational hourly rate. 15:34 So you study information that drastically increases your earning potential. 15:37 You need to self educate practice skills that make you self-reliant. 15:41 Start a business. 15:42 Join a startup, push for an executive role. 15:45 You just learn and you build. 15:46 And the more you think about your actions now, 15:49 the more you will realize why you're not rich. 15:52 And then you can change that.

sections.title: Give yourself no other option but to succeed
sections.content: 15:54 Now, the last and final point for how to get rich, 15:57 is to give yourself no other option but to succeed, 16:00 because the best decisions I've made always involve, 16:03 using money as a forcing function to make more. 16:07 In other words, I invested in things that I couldn't afford, 16:10 and I learned to trust myself to make that money back much faster. 16:15 I always talk about the story of how I moved into an apartment, 16:19 that was two times what I could afford to kind of go all in on my business, 16:23 and force myself to make more and be comfortable with that amount. 16:27 But I actually did this one time before, around, 16:30 when my business was first starting, and I wanted to invest, 16:35 in this email marketing software that was like $1,500 a year. 16:38 And at the time, my business wasn't doing that good like that was big. 16:43 That was a lot of money to me, and it deeply pained me, 16:46 to see $1,500 go out of my bank account, 16:49 to invest in something that I didn't even know if I could make it work. 16:53 But I trusted myself and I paid for the software. 16:57 And what that did was I felt this pressure, this good pressure, 17:01 this good stress to learn the skills and acquire, 17:04 the knowledge that would allow me to maximize the use of that software. 17:08 So that meant learning things like landing page design, funnel design, 17:12 copywriting, and those things paid off massively. 17:15 Most normal people only see money as a way to survive or to buy nice things. 17:21 They don't see it as a way to create, 17:23 pressure for you to become a better version of yourself. 17:27 And when it came to moving into the apartments that I couldn't afford, 17:31 on the surface it looked like I was just another victim of lifestyle creep. 17:35 Like people. 17:35 Still, it's actually really annoying and you just have to brush it off, 17:39 like people see what you're doing, like friends and family, 17:43 and they don't even have to say it for you to understand what they think. 17:47 They're just like, oh, you're stupid. 17:48 Why would you make that stupid decision? 17:50 What they don't understand is that most of the time. 17:53 when I was making those irresponsible purchases, 17:56 I was imposing a deadline on a challenge, 17:59 that I knew I could take on, 18:01 that would force me to learn skills and acquire knowledge, 18:05 so that that purchase was no longer irrational or stupid. 18:09 I was physically putting myself in the environment, 18:13 of my future self and allowing time to bridge the gap. 18:17 So those three decisions an aspirational hourly rate, 18:21 concentration of force, and giving myself no other option, 18:24 but to succeed created the perfect conditions, 18:27 to make more money than my younger self thought was possible. 18:30 None of those involved obsessing over, 18:33 what skill should I learn or what business model should I choose? 18:37 I didn't have time to think about that. 18:40 I simply had to make the decisions that aligned with my hourly rate, 18:43 force me to focus on my goal, and put me in a situation, 18:48 where I had no other option but to make it work. 18:50 With those three things, it's, 18:52 it becomes very obvious, 18:54 what skills you should learn, what business model you should try. 18:57 It doesn't really matter for any of those, 18:59 because it's not like you're learning one skill. 19:01 You don't take one class in college and expect yourself to be good. 19:05 You continue learning skills for the entirety of your life. 19:08 So if you haven't started, 19:10 that snowball process of skill acquisition and knowledge acquisition, 19:13 you aren't learning and building every day, 19:15 then you're just delaying the amount of time it will take to get rich. 19:18 It doesn't matter what business model you choose or which one you start with, 19:22 because they all work. 19:23 The first one is just a prototype. 19:25 You try it, it's probably going to fail, but then you correct yourself. 19:29 Next time you choose a different one and slowly, 19:32 you understand what you want to do, and then you do it. 19:34 Now, the thing here is that I'm not a proponent, 19:37 of getting rich quick, but you would be surprised how quick, 19:41 you can get rich with the conditions that you said, 19:43 that we went over in this video. 19:45 In fact, I would argue that you can learn any skill in two weeks. 19:49 And that's what we're going to talk about, 19:51 in next week's video. So subscribe for that. 19:53 Like the video because you're already here, 19:55 and the buttons right there. So just click it. 19:57 I would appreciate it and join my Substack, 20:00 link in the description for weekly letters on human, 20:03 potential, business future proofing yourself, 20:06 so on and so forth. Thank you for watching this video. 20:08 I'll see you in the next one. Bye. --- 由 vCaptions 生成 ---

excerpt: 00:00 The only way to get rich is to one value your time at a delusional dollar amount, 00:06 to be obsessed with getting rich, and three, 00:09 give yourself no other option but to get rich. 00:13 That's what this entire video is about, is getting rich and the truth behind it. 00:18 And now I know...
